skill.name: senior-prompt-engineer
skill.description: Advanced prompt engineering guidance for LLM optimization, structured outputs, evaluation design, RAG workflows, and agent architecture planning.
skill.summary: Optimize prompts, evaluations, RAG flows, and agent designs for production AI systems.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, and shell execution patterns, but review found these are false positives in generic documentation, parser descriptions, and Markdown command examples. The Python scripts are simple stubs that parse arguments and print status JSON; no network calls, shell execution, credential access, weak crypto APIs, or prompt injection attempts were found. Low residual risk remains because the skill includes runnable example commands that users should review before execution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Network Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited Markdown lines contain headings, generic design guidance, and resource references. The cited Python lines are argparse description strings. I found no weak cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, socket scan, host discovery, or network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location was reviewed in context. The matches are plain documentation text or CLI help strings, not executable security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Not Auto-Executed
low_findings.description: The shell-related alerts point to fenced Markdown examples and inline file paths. They document optional local commands and deployment tools, but the skill does not execute them automatically. Users should still review commands before copy-pasting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are inside SKILL.md documentation, not runtime code. Residual risk is limited to user-initiated execution of documented examples.

content.value_statement: Teams often ship prompts without clear evaluation, structure, or operational guardrails. This skill provides practical prompt patterns, RAG review guidance, and agent design checklists for production AI work.
actual_capabilities: Provides prompt engineering patterns for structured outputs, few-shot examples, and chain-of-thought planning.
Guides evaluation design for LLM quality, reliability, and regression testing.
Reviews RAG workflows for retrieval quality, context construction, and response assessment.
Supports agent architecture planning with orchestration, monitoring, and deployment considerations.
Includes stub Python CLIs for prompt optimization, RAG evaluation, and agent orchestration workflows.
Summarizes production concerns such as latency, throughput, availability, and compliance.
limitations: The included Python scripts are scaffolds and do not implement full optimization or evaluation logic.
The reference documents are high-level guides, not complete implementation manuals.
It does not validate live model outputs unless users add project-specific evaluators.
Deployment commands are examples and require review for each environment.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Prompts
use_cases.description: Refine customer-facing prompts with clearer roles, constraints, examples, and output expectations.

use_cases.title: Design LLM Evaluations
use_cases.description: Create repeatable checks for quality, grounding, safety, latency, and regression behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Map agent responsibilities, tool boundaries, monitoring needs, and deployment risks before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a Basic Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this prompt for clarity and reliability. Keep the original goal, add missing constraints, and specify the expected output format: [paste prompt].

prompt_templates.title: Add Evaluation Criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Design evaluation criteria for this LLM task. Include success criteria, failure modes, sample test cases, and review metrics: [describe task].

prompt_templates.title: Improve a RAG Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this RAG workflow for retrieval quality, context assembly, grounding checks, and answer evaluation. Recommend concrete changes: [describe workflow].

prompt_templates.title: Architect an Agent System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an agentic system for this use case. Define roles, tools, guardrails, state handling, observability, evaluation, and deployment risks: [describe use case].

output_examples.input: A support chatbot prompt that gives inconsistent refund answers.
output_examples.output: A revised prompt brief with role definition, policy boundaries, refusal behavior, examples, and evaluation checks for refund accuracy.

output_examples.input: A RAG assistant with high hallucination rates on internal documents.
output_examples.output: A review plan covering retrieval diagnostics, context ranking, citation checks, abstention rules, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: A proposed multi-agent workflow for incident response summaries.
output_examples.output: An architecture outline with agent roles, tool permissions, handoff rules, audit logging, and production readiness concerns.

best_practices: Pair every prompt change with evaluation cases that cover normal, edge, and failure scenarios.
Keep tool permissions, context sources, and output schemas explicit in production prompts.
Review documented shell commands before running them in a real repository or cluster.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on vague instructions when the task needs a strict output format.
Do not deploy agent workflows without tool boundaries, logging, and rollback plans.
Do not treat the included scaffold scripts as complete production automation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It contains Markdown command examples and Python script scaffolds, but execution requires a user action.

faq.question: Can it improve prompts for Claude and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is model-agnostic and includes workflows useful with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include complete evaluation software?
faq.answer: No. It provides evaluation structure and stub scripts, but teams must add project-specific tests and metrics.

faq.question: Is it useful for RAG systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers retrieval quality, context construction, grounding checks, and response evaluation.

faq.question: Can it design multi-agent systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps plan agent roles, orchestration, monitoring, deployment, and operational risks.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing work from it?
faq.answer: Users should review commands, security assumptions, evaluation coverage, and environment-specific deployment details.
